channel.title: Biotech for Normal People
channel.description: Biotech for Normal People is AI-produced. The research, scripts, and voices are all generated by AI. The science is real. Every episode draws from some of the most cited and influential peer-reviewed research in the field, sourced from PubMed, with DOI links in every set of show notes. We cover the biggest ideas in biotech, gene editing, GLP-1s, AI drug discovery, cancer immunotherapy, and more, and translate them into plain English for people who are curious but not scientists. No PhD required. No pretending otherwise.
channel.itunes:author: Biotech for Normal People
channel.itunes:summary: Biotech for Normal People is AI-produced. The research, scripts, and voices are all generated by AI. The science is real. Every episode draws from some of the most cited and influential peer-reviewed research in the field, sourced from PubMed, with DOI links in every set of show notes. We cover the biggest ideas in biotech, gene editing, GLP-1s, AI drug discovery, cancer immunotherapy, and more, and translate them into plain English for people who are curious but not scientists. No PhD required. No pretending otherwise.

item.title: Episode 01: What Are GLP-1s?
item.description: The complete story of GLP-1 receptor agonists: from a Gila monster’s venom and incretin biology to Ozempic, tirzepatide, and the future of metabolic medicine. We explain what peptides are, how these drugs work in the body, the difference between GLP-1 and GIP, why dual agonists outperform single ones, what the cardiovascular trial data actually shows, and where triple agonists are headed.
item.content:encoded:
The complete story of GLP-1 receptor agonists: from a Gila monster’s venom and incretin biology to Ozempic, tirzepatide, and the future of metabolic medicine. We explain what peptides are, how these drugs work in the body, the difference between GLP-1 and GIP, why dual agonists outperform single ones, what the cardiovascular trial data actually shows, and where triple agonists are headed.

Source Papers

  • Muller et al. (2019) — Glucagon-like peptide 1 (GLP-1)
  • Nauck & D’Alessio (2022) — Tirzepatide dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist
  • Liu (2024) — Mechanisms of GLP-1 and dual GIP/GLP-1 agonists
  • Kristensen et al. (2019) — CV/mortality/kidney outcomes meta-analysis (56,004 patients)
  • Snell-Bergeon et al. (2025) — Off-label semaglutide/tirzepatide in Type 1 diabetes

All sources retrieved from PubMed (National Library of Medicine).

About This Podcast

Biotech for Normal People is 100% AI-produced — the research, script, and voices you hear are all generated by AI tools. The science is real. Every claim is sourced from peer-reviewed literature, linked above. The goal is simple: there is too much important research that never reaches normal people. We are not trying to fool anyone. We are trying to make the science accessible.

Produced by Biotech for Normal People | Catalyst Collective LLC.
item.itunes:title: Episode 01: What Are GLP-1s?
item.itunes:summary: The complete story of GLP-1 receptor agonists: from a Gila monster’s venom and incretin biology to Ozempic, tirzepatide, and the future of metabolic medicine. We explain what peptides are, how these drugs work in the body, the difference between GLP-1 and GIP, why dual agonists outperform single ones, what the cardiovascular trial data actually shows, and where triple agonists are headed.
item.itunes:keywords: GLP-1, Ozempic, semaglutide, tirzepatide, metabolic medicine, peptides, biotech, obesity, diabetes

item.title: Episode 0: What This Is
item.description: A quick explanation of what Biotech for Normal People is, how it's made, and why we're being upfront about it.
item.content:encoded:
A quick explanation of what Biotech for Normal People is, how it's made, and why we're being upfront about it.

About This Podcast

Biotech for Normal People is 100% AI-produced — the research, script, and voices you hear are all generated by AI tools. The science is real. Every claim is sourced from peer-reviewed literature, linked above. The goal is simple: there is too much important research that never reaches normal people. We are not trying to fool anyone. We are trying to make the science accessible.

Produced by Biotech for Normal People | Catalyst Collective LLC.

item.itunes:summary: A quick explanation of what Biotech for Normal People is, how it's made, and why we're being upfront about it.
